title: Lesson 17 — Weathervane

words.example: Fashion is mutable, shifting with every season.

words.definition: unchanging over time; unable to be changed
words.example: The immutable laws of physics govern every experiment.

words.definition: subject to sudden or unpredictable changes of mood
words.example: A mercurial boss, cheerful one minute and furious the next.

words.definition: liable to change rapidly and unpredictably, often for the worse
words.example: A volatile market that swung wildly all week.

words.definition: able to change frequently or easily; versatile
words.example: A protean actor who vanished into every role.

words.definition: changing frequently, especially in loyalty or affection
words.example: Fickle fans abandoned the team after a single loss.
A steadfast friend who stayed through every hardship.
A staid old firm that never took a risk.

words.definition: not flowing or changing; showing no activity or growth
words.example: A stagnant economy that created no new jobs.

words.definition: to waver between different opinions or courses of action
words.example: He vacillated for weeks before finally deciding.

words.definition: to become unsteady or irresolute; to falter
words.example: Her resolve never wavered, even under pressure.

words.definition: to swing back and forth; to vary between extremes
words.example: His mood oscillated between hope and despair.

story: The market was [[mutable|mutable]] where the founder wished it were [[immutable|immutable]]. Investors proved [[mercurial|mercurial]], the tech press [[volatile|volatile]], and public taste as [[protean|protean]] as it was [[fickle|fickle]]. Through it all his cofounder stayed [[steadfast|steadfast]] — reassuringly [[staid|staid]], some said dull — even as the product itself seemed to grow [[stagnant|stagnant]]. The founder began to [[vacillate|vacillate]]: some mornings his confidence would [[waver|waver]], and his plans would [[oscillate|oscillate]] between bold expansion and quiet retreat.
